title: Best Affiliate Tracking Software in 2026: Compared and Ranked
excerpt: If you don't track, you don't know what's working. Here are the best affiliate tracking tools in 2026 — from solo marketers to enterprise networks.
seoDescription: The best affiliate tracking software in 2026 compared — Voluum, ClickMagick, Everflow, Trackdesk, and more. Find the right tool for your setup.
body:
Most affiliate marketers optimize by gut feel. They write more content in the niches that seem to be working, promote products that "feel" like they convert, and wonder why their income plateaus. The ones who consistently scale all share one thing: they track everything. Here are the tools worth using in 2026.

## Voluum — Enterprise-Scale Tracking

Voluum is the most widely used affiliate tracking platform globally, with over 100,000 active campaigns and more than 500 million clicks processed monthly. It tracks the full conversion journey across paid and organic channels, provides real-time reporting, and integrates with virtually every traffic source.

Voluum's strength is multi-step funnel tracking — you can measure performance at every stage from ad impression through to final purchase, including upsells and order bumps. It supports postback URLs, which are essential for accurate tracking across affiliate networks that don't use cookies.

Pricing starts at $69/month for the basic plan. It's overkill for a solo blogger running SEO content; it's indispensable for anyone running paid traffic to affiliate offers.

## ClickMagick — Built for Solo Affiliates

ClickMagick is a link tracking and funnel analytics tool designed specifically for individual affiliate marketers and small teams. It provides click tracking, conversion tracking, A/B link testing, and basic funnel analytics without the complexity of enterprise platforms.

The bot filtering feature alone justifies the cost for many affiliates — it removes bot and proxy traffic from your stats so you're making decisions based on real human clicks. Pricing starts at $37/month.

Best for: solo affiliates running content sites, email campaigns, or small-scale paid traffic who need accurate click attribution without a steep learning curve.

## Everflow — For Networks and Agencies

Everflow is a performance marketing platform built for affiliate networks and sophisticated brands. It tracks clicks, conversions, and post-conversion events (upsells, subscriptions, repeat purchases) across affiliates, referral programs, paid ads, and offline channels.

The flowchart-style reporting is genuinely useful — it maps the full conversion path from first click to final action in a visual format. Everflow is also API-first, which matters if you're building custom dashboards or automating campaign management.

Pricing is custom (enterprise-level). Not for individual affiliates, but worth knowing if you're building or joining a network.

## Trackdesk — Volume and Reliability

Trackdesk is built for volume: unlimited clicks, conversions, and affiliates across all plans, with 99.99% uptime on Google Cloud infrastructure. It covers fraud protection, bulk automated payouts, real-time analytics, and 900+ integrations.

> What doesn't get tracked doesn't get optimized. A $37/month tracking tool that saves you from a single bad campaign decision pays for itself in an afternoon.

## Cometly — For Multi-Channel Attribution

Cometly is an AI-powered marketing attribution platform that tracks the full customer journey across paid ads, affiliate channels, and organic sources simultaneously. It's designed for teams running paid ads alongside affiliate programs who need to understand true cross-channel ROI.

If you're spending on Google Ads, Meta Ads, and running affiliate programs simultaneously, Cometly's unified attribution prevents double-counting and mis-attribution that distorts optimization decisions.

## What to Track From Day One

Even if you're not ready for paid tracking tools, set up UTM parameters on every affiliate link you place. UTMs pass traffic source data through to Google Analytics so you can see which posts, channels, and campaigns are driving your actual affiliate conversions.

The metrics that matter: click-through rate on affiliate links (tells you if your placement and copy are working), earnings per click (tells you if the product and audience match), and conversion rate by traffic source (tells you where to invest more). Track these from the beginning and you'll never make major optimization decisions blind.
